discount_pct: Varies — typically 20–30% below retail after tax savings
desc: The military's own retail network offers tax-free shopping, exclusive military pricing, and profits return to MWR programs. NEXCOM (Navy), AAFES (Army/Air Force), MCX (Marines), and CGX (Coast Guard) are all connected online at ShopMyExchange.com. Available worldwide at installations and online.
how_to_verify: Military ID (CAC, dependent ID) or ShopMyExchange.com account with verified military status.
notes: Online exchange access extended to all honorably discharged veterans in 2020 (online only for veterans).

desc: 10% off all regular, sale, and clearance items in-store. Requires verification via ID.me or GovX at checkout. Maximum $400 discount per transaction. Active, veterans, retirees, and spouses all eligible. Available in all U.S. stores.
how_to_verify: ID.me or GovX verification. Present QR code at register or link to Home Depot account online.
notes: One of the most commonly used retail military discounts. Major benefit for homeowners — 10% on large purchases adds up fast.

desc: 10% off eligible purchases. Must verify via Lowe's MyLowe's account linked to military status through ID.me. Available in-store and online. No published dollar cap on discount (some exclusions apply).
how_to_verify: Verify at Lowes.com/military via ID.me. Link to MyLowe's account for ongoing in-store and online discount.
notes: Lowe's and Home Depot discounts are competitive — Lowe's has no stated cap, which matters on large appliance or lumber purchases.

desc: 10% off most products via Best Buy Military program. Requires ID.me verification. Apply through BestBuy.com or in store. Excludes certain items including some Apple products, Sony PlayStation, and items already on sale.
how_to_verify: Register at BestBuy.com/military and verify via ID.me. Digital coupon applied at checkout.
notes: 10% on electronics is significant. Exclusions on some brands — verify at checkout.

desc: Apple's Veterans Advantage program offers discounts on Mac, iPad, and accessories through the Apple Store for Military. Discounts vary by product. Available online and at Apple retail stores. Some products (iPhone) typically excluded.
how_to_verify: Verify through Veterans Advantage (GovX) at apple.com/shop/go/product/veterans_advantage or at an Apple Store with military ID.
notes: Discount is on Mac computers, iPads, and accessories primarily. iPhones typically sold at carrier-subsidized prices without additional military discount.

desc: Dell's Member Purchase Program (MPP) for military offers discounts up to 15% on consumer and business laptops, desktops, monitors, and peripherals. Stackable with Dell sales promotions in some cases.
how_to_verify: Verify at Dell.com/en-us/work/shop/overview/cp/military-discount. Requires ID.me verification.
notes: One of the better electronics discounts. Dell's MPP frequently runs alongside other promotional pricing.

desc: Microsoft offers military discounts on Surface devices and accessories through the Microsoft Store. ID.me verification required. Also provides free Microsoft 365 for active duty through some base programs and partnerships.
how_to_verify: Visit Microsoft.com/military or Microsoft Store. ID.me verification.
notes: Surface hardware discount is the primary benefit. Software (Office/Microsoft 365) may be separately available free through base IT programs.

desc: Nike offers a 10% military discount online via ID.me and in select Nike retail stores. Applies to regular-priced items; may exclude items already on sale. Verify at Nike.com/military.
how_to_verify: Verify at Nike.com/help/a/military-discount via ID.me. Digital code applied at checkout. In-store: present military ID.
notes: Nike discount applies to Nike.com and Nike app. Not all Nike Clearance or outlet stores participate.

desc: Under Armour's Heroes program offers 20% off regular-priced items via GovX or ID.me. One of the most generous apparel discounts in the military benefits space. Applies at UA.com and in select UA Brand Houses.
how_to_verify: Register at UA.com/heroes via GovX or ID.me. Apply discount code at checkout.
notes: 20% is notably higher than most retail military discounts. Can be very valuable for PT gear, tactical footwear, and cold-weather layers.

desc: REI offers a 10% military discount via GovX ID. Additionally, REI waives the $30 lifetime co-op membership fee for active and veteran military. The membership itself entitles members to annual dividends on purchases.
how_to_verify: Register at REI.com/military via GovX. Waived membership can be applied in store or online.
notes: The free $30 membership plus 10% discount plus annual member dividend makes this one of the better retail combinations for outdoor enthusiasts.

desc: Bass Pro and Cabela's (same parent company) offer military discounts on hunting, fishing, camping, and outdoor gear. Discount amount varies by item category. Present military ID or DD-214 in store.
how_to_verify: Military ID or DD-214 at checkout. Online: varies — check current program status at BassProShops.com.
notes: Discount percentage varies. In-store is most reliable. Online discount program has had periods of suspension — verify before visiting.

desc: Ace Hardware offers a 10% Veterans Advantage discount at participating locations. As a franchise, participation varies by store location. Present military ID or verify through ID.me.
how_to_verify: Military ID at register or ID.me verification. Call ahead to confirm local participation.
notes: Franchise model means not all locations participate. High-participation rates in areas near military installations.

desc: Costco offers discounted or free Gold Star ($65/yr) membership to qualifying military through various programs. Additionally, the military commissary system (DeCA) offers unmatched savings on groceries — averaging 23–25% below retail — for ID cardholders worldwide.
how_to_verify: GovX or ID.me for Costco discount verification. Commissary access via military ID card.
notes: The commissary system (DeCA) is the bigger benefit here — 23–25% below retail on groceries is substantial for families.

desc: The commissary system provides authorized patrons with groceries at cost plus a 5% surcharge — averaging 23–25% savings over commercial grocery stores. Over 240 locations worldwide including OCONUS. SNAP accepted. Benefit extended to all honorably discharged veterans in 2020.
how_to_verify: Military ID card at commissary entrance. Online ordering available at commissaries.com for pickup.
notes: For a family spending $800/month on groceries, 23% savings equals ~$2,200/year. One of the highest-value passive financial benefits in the military system.

desc: Denny's offers a free Grand Slam breakfast (6am–noon) to active and veteran military on Veterans Day annually. All locations participating. No purchase required; military ID, DD-214, or VA letter accepted as proof of service.
how_to_verify: Military ID, DD-214, VA card, or discharge papers at host stand on Veterans Day.
notes: One of the original Veterans Day restaurant programs — running for 15+ years.

desc: Texas Roadhouse offers a free lunch from their special Veterans Day menu every November 11th (11am–2pm). All veterans welcome — no purchase required. Military ID, DD-214, VA card, or honorable discharge papers accepted. One of the most popular Veterans Day dining deals nationally.
how_to_verify: Military ID, DD-214, or honorable discharge paperwork at host stand. Lunch window: 11am–2pm.
notes: Texas Roadhouse is one of the most consistently delivered Veterans Day benefits. Long lines are common — arrive early.

desc: Applebee's annual Veterans Day free meal features select entrées at no charge for veterans and active duty. Requires proof of service. Dine-in only. Applebee's has run this program for over 20 consecutive years.
how_to_verify: Military ID, DD-214, VA card, or other proof of military service at host stand.
notes: Dine-in only. The specific menu items offered vary slightly year to year but typically include burgers, pasta, and chicken dishes.

desc: Cracker Barrel honors veterans with a free Double Meat Country Boy Breakfast on Veterans Day. Also offers a year-round 10% military discount at many participating locations. Military ID required.
how_to_verify: Military ID at server or host stand. Year-round discount: ask server at time of order.
notes: Year-round 10% discount is the more broadly applicable benefit here. Franchised locations may vary — confirm locally.

desc: Golden Corral's Military Appreciation Monday (the Monday after Veterans Day each year) offers a free dinner buffet to all veterans and active duty. No purchase required. Typically serves 500,000–1,000,000 meals nationally. One of the largest single-day veteran benefit programs in the country.
how_to_verify: Military ID, DD-214, VA card, or honorable discharge. No appointment needed.
notes: Typically held the Monday after Veterans Day, not November 11 itself. Expect long lines — arrive at opening.

discount: Free Tall Coffee (Veterans Day) + Military Family Stores
discount_pct: 100% Veterans Day; select year-round discounts at Military Family Stores
desc: Starbucks offers a free tall coffee to active military and veterans on Veterans Day. Year-round, Starbucks operates Military Family Stores near installations (250+ locations) staffed by veteran spouses and partners, and offers hiring preferences and support programs for the military community.
how_to_verify: Military ID or DD-214 on Veterans Day. Military Family Store locations may offer year-round discounts — check with your local store.
notes: The Military Family Stores program also means Starbucks actively hires veteran spouses — worth mentioning to military family members seeking employment.

desc: IHOP traditionally offers free red, white, and blue pancakes to veterans and active military on Veterans Day. Participating locations across the U.S. Military ID, DD-214, or VA ID accepted.
how_to_verify: Military ID or DD-214 at host stand on Veterans Day.
notes: Participation varies by franchise location — call ahead to confirm.

discount: Government / Military Rate (15–25% off rack rate)
desc: Marriott offers military and government rates at most properties worldwide across all portfolio brands: Marriott, Westin, Sheraton, W Hotels, Courtyard, Residence Inn, Ritz-Carlton, and more. Book via Marriott.com/government or call reservations with rate code GOV.
how_to_verify: Military ID at check-in. Book using government/military rate online — no advance verification required, but ID checked at property.
notes: Government rate availability varies by property and date. Some high-demand properties or dates may not have government inventory available. Always compare to AAA or member rates.

desc: Hilton Honors members in the military receive up to 100,000 bonus points for deployment. Government/military rates available at Hilton, Hampton Inn, Embassy Suites, DoubleTree, Curio, and other portfolio brands. Active duty Hilton Honors points don't expire during deployment.
how_to_verify: Book at Hilton.com/government. Military ID at check-in. Deployment bonus points: submit claim through Hilton Honors account.
notes: The 100,000 deployment points bonus is a rarely-advertised benefit worth ~$500–$1,000 in hotel stays. Worth claiming after every deployment.

desc: IHG offers government/military rates at Holiday Inn, Holiday Inn Express, Crowne Plaza, InterContinental, Staybridge Suites, Candlewood Suites, and other brands worldwide. IHG Rewards points don't expire for active duty members. Particularly strong in Europe, Middle East, and Asia-Pacific.
how_to_verify: Book at IHG.com using government rate. Military ID at check-in.
notes: IHG has strong coverage in OCONUS locations. Particularly useful in Germany, UK, Japan, and Middle East where IHG properties are common.

desc: Wyndham offers military rates at Days Inn, Super 8, La Quinta, Ramada, Howard Johnson, Microtel, and other portfolio brands. Use rate code MIL or present military ID at check-in. Good for domestic travel on a budget.
how_to_verify: Rate code MIL at Wyndham.com or present military ID at check-in.
notes: Wyndham's portfolio is budget-to-mid-scale. Useful for road trips and domestic TDY stays.

desc: AFVC offers space-available stays at 4,000+ resort properties worldwide for as little as $329/week per unit. Properties include resort condominiums, beach villas, and mountain cabins sleeping 4–10 people. Huge value for families who can be flexible on dates — often booking 2–4 weeks before travel.
how_to_verify: Register free at AFVClub.com with military ID. Book available units based on eligibility.
notes: The key to AFVC is flexibility — the best units go fast, but last-minute availability is common. Units can sleep 6–10, making the per-person cost extraordinary.

desc: American Forces Travel (powered by Priceline for military) offers exclusive military hotel, car rental, and cruise deals. Access via AmericanForcesTravel.com. Free to use with military ID.me verification. Often competitive with or better than public Priceline rates.
how_to_verify: Register at AmericanForcesTravel.com with ID.me military verification.
notes: Useful for hotel and car rental bookings. Compare rates with Marriott/Hilton government rates before booking to find the best price.

desc: Vet Tix provides free tickets to sporting events, concerts, performing arts, NFL/MLB/NBA/NHL games, and more. Completely free to register. Tickets donated by teams and venues. Over 5 million tickets distributed to date. One of the most underutilized veteran benefits available.
how_to_verify: Register free at VetTix.org with proof of military service (DD-214, CAC, or VA card).
notes: Registration is fast and free. Ticket availability varies by market — high-demand areas (major sports cities) have more options. Sign up immediately.

desc: Ford's Military Appreciation Program offers $500–$1,500+ off select new Ford vehicles including F-Series trucks, Mustang, Explorer, Bronco, and Escape. Stackable with most national Ford incentives. Present military ID at dealership or verify online within 180 days of purchase.
how_to_verify: Present military ID at dealership. Combine with current Ford incentives for maximum savings. Certificate issued at Ford.com/military.
notes: F-150 and Super Duty truck rebates can be combined with X-Plan pricing for total savings of $2,000–$5,000+ depending on model and timing.

desc: General Motors' Military Discount Program applies across Chevrolet, GMC, Buick, and Cadillac. Bonus cash of $500–$1,000+ on select models. Stackable with other GM incentives. Verify eligibility at GM Military Discount portal and present at dealership.
how_to_verify: Register at GM Military Discount portal. Certificate presented at GM dealership.
notes: Silverado, Sierra, and Tahoe are popular choices. Truck purchases benefit most from stacked GM incentives.

desc: Toyota's Military Rebate Program offers $500 toward the purchase or lease of most new Toyota vehicles. Stackable with most current national Toyota incentives. Verify within 180 days of vehicle purchase. Valid for up to 2 vehicles per year.
how_to_verify: Present military ID at Toyota dealership. Certificate available at Toyota.com/military.
notes: Toyota 4Runner, Tacoma, and Tundra are particularly popular with military buyers. $500 is modest but stackable.

desc: USAA's Car Buying Service connects members with TrueCar-certified dealers offering guaranteed below-invoice pricing. Average savings of $3,500 below MSRP. Free service for USAA members. Stacks with manufacturer military rebates for maximum savings.
how_to_verify: Log into USAA.com and navigate to Auto Circle. TrueCar certificate generated for your specific vehicle choice.
notes: This is one of the most financially significant auto benefits: combine USAA Auto Circle (TrueCar below invoice) + manufacturer military rebate + USAA low-rate auto loan for the lowest possible vehicle acquisition cost.

desc: T-Mobile offers the Magenta Military plan with 2 lines for $80/month taxes included, 4 lines for $100/month. Includes Netflix Basic, 5GB mobile hotspot, international texting/data in 210+ countries, and HD streaming. Also offers 55+ veteran plan option.
how_to_verify: Military ID or ID.me verification at T-Mobile store or T-Mobile.com/military.
notes: T-Mobile's military plan is frequently cited as the best-value carrier plan for military families. Netflix inclusion alone offsets a significant portion of the plan cost.

desc: Verizon offers a 15% military discount on monthly service for eligible members. Applies to most plan types including unlimited. Also offers extended payment deferrals for deployed members and international plan benefits for OCONUS duty.
how_to_verify: Verify at Verizon.com/military via ID.me or in-store with military ID.
notes: Verizon's network coverage is strongest for rural U.S. locations. For OCONUS-stationed personnel, confirm international plan options before PCSing.

desc: AT&T offers 25% off eligible unlimited plans through the AT&T Military program. One of the highest single-line carrier discounts. Includes AT&T ActiveArmor mobile security. Requires ID.me or myAT&T verification.
how_to_verify: Verify at AT&T.com/military or in-store via ID.me.
notes: AT&T has strong coverage in regions where T-Mobile may be weaker. 25% off is a compelling discount for existing AT&T customers.

desc: Xfinity offers discounts on internet service for military households and provides account freeze/pause options for deployed members without cancellation fees or continued billing. Also offers free Xfinity WiFi hotspot access for deployed members' families.
how_to_verify: Call Xfinity customer service with military ID information, or visit Xfinity.com/military.
notes: The deployment account freeze is the most valuable feature — prevents $1,000+ in unnecessary charges during a 6–9 month deployment.

discount: Full financial services suite — best-in-class for military
desc: USAA is the gold standard for military financial services. Offers competitive auto/home/life/renters insurance, no-fee checking with ATM fee reimbursement worldwide, VA and conventional mortgages, auto loans, personal loans, and investment brokerage. Eligibility extended to spouses, children, and widows/widowers of USAA members.
how_to_verify: Eligibility verified at USAA.com. CAC, DD-214, or service branch verification.
notes: USAA consistently receives highest customer satisfaction scores among financial institutions. Auto insurance for military often runs 10–20% below competitors. The overseas ATM fee reimbursement is critical for OCONUS duty.

discount: Best-in-class loan rates and financial products
desc: Navy Federal is the world's largest credit union, open to all DoD, Coast Guard, and Intelligence Community members and their families. Offers VA mortgages with competitive rates, auto loans frequently beating dealer rates, the Flagship Rewards Visa (3x points on travel), and free checking with no minimums.
how_to_verify: Eligible via military branch, DoD civilian status, or family member of existing NFCU member. Apply at NavyFederal.org.
notes: NFCU auto loan rates are frequently 1–3% below what car dealerships offer. On a $40,000 car loan, that equals $1,000–$3,000+ in interest savings. Always check NFCU before accepting dealer financing.

desc: PenFed offers competitive auto loan rates (often among the nation's lowest), VA and conventional mortgages, and the Platinum Rewards Visa Signature card. Free military membership for all U.S. armed forces. High-yield savings accounts and certificate rates.
how_to_verify: Apply at PenFed.org with military service documentation.
notes: PenFed and Navy Federal have competitive auto loan rates — check both before financing a vehicle. PenFed sometimes leads on new auto loan rates.

discount: 100% Free Federal + State Tax Filing (no income limits)
desc: MilTax (via Military OneSource) provides completely free federal and state tax preparation software AND live tax consultant support. No income limits, no EFile fees, no upsells. Equivalent to TurboTax Premier ($90–$150 commercial). Available year-round, not just during tax season.
how_to_verify: Access via MilitaryOneSource.mil with CAC or DS Logon. No additional verification needed.
notes: Completely free including state returns in all 50 states. Live consultant support is available by phone and video for complex tax situations including PCS moves, combat pay exclusions, and OCONUS filing.

discount: 6% interest rate cap + multiple legal protections
discount_pct: Savings depend on pre-service debt load
desc: The SCRA caps interest rates at 6% on pre-service debts (mortgages, credit cards, auto loans, student loans) during active duty. Also provides eviction protection, lease termination rights (vehicle and housing), court stay provisions, and protection from default judgments. Applies automatically — but you must notify lenders with a copy of orders.
how_to_verify: Send copy of active duty orders to lenders by certified mail or via SCRA.dmdc.osd.mil. DoJ SCRA hotline: 800-253-3931.
notes: On a $200,000 mortgage at 7%, SCRA reduction to 6% saves $2,000/year. On credit card debt at 20%+, savings are even more dramatic. SCRA is automatic by law — lenders are required to comply.

discount: 36% MAPR cap on new consumer loans
desc: The Military Lending Act caps the Military Annual Percentage Rate (MAPR) at 36% on most new consumer credit products for active duty and their dependents. Prohibits prepayment penalties, mandatory arbitration clauses, and balloon payments on covered products. Protects against payday lenders, title loans, and tax refund anticipation loans near bases.
how_to_verify: Applies automatically to eligible servicemembers. Lenders check MLA status via DMDC.osd.mil database.
notes: MLA applies to NEW credit products taken out after October 2007. It does not cover pre-existing debts (SCRA covers those). Key protection against predatory lenders near military bases.

desc: The only hotel located inside Walt Disney World exclusively for the military. Located between the Grand Floridian and Polynesian Resorts. Free Disney transportation to all parks, discounted park tickets at front desk, early park entry. Shades of Green is arguably the single most valuable military travel benefit in the U.S.
how_to_verify: Active military ID or DoD credentials at hotel check-in. Book at ShadesOfGreen.org.
notes: Book exactly at your 18-month window — peak dates (spring break, summer, Christmas) sell out within hours.

org: Disney Theme Parks — Military Salute Program
desc: Disney's Military Salute program offers 4-day or 6-day park-hopper or single-park tickets at deeply discounted rates compared to the standard $109–$189/day gate price. Available annually (typically Jan–Sept with dates adjusted each year). Purchased at Shades of Green front desk, Disneyworld.com/military, or base ITT offices. Each active duty member can purchase up to 6 tickets per offer period.
how_to_verify: CAC or military ID. Purchase at ShadesOfGreen.org, DisneyWorld.com/military, or base ITT/ITR office.
notes: Disney Military Salute tickets are often the cheapest legal way to buy Disney park tickets. Up to 6 tickets can be purchased per active duty service member per offer period, including for family and friends.

discount: Free 1-day ticket (Active Duty); discounted for Vets/Retirees
desc: Universal offers active military free 1-day tickets to both Universal Studios Florida and Islands of Adventure annually. Veterans and retirees receive discounted park tickets. Tickets available at USO locations, ITT offices, and Universal's military ticket portal.
how_to_verify: CAC or military ID. Free tickets obtained via Universal military portal or base ITT office.
notes: Free tickets include both Universal Studios Florida and Islands of Adventure — two parks for free. Includes access to Wizarding World of Harry Potter.

org: SeaWorld / Busch Gardens — Waves of Honor
desc: SeaWorld's Waves of Honor program offers free single-day admission to active military at SeaWorld Orlando, San Diego, San Antonio, Busch Gardens Tampa, Busch Gardens Williamsburg, Aquatica, and Sesame Place. Up to 3 free admissions per year for the active duty member. Up to 3 accompanying guests receive 50% off admission.
how_to_verify: Register at SeaWorld.com/military. CAC or military ID required at gate. Online registration recommended to avoid lines.
notes: Up to 3 free admissions per year (not per visit) for the active duty member. 50% off for up to 3 guests is a separate significant discount.

desc: Six Flags offers complimentary admission to active duty military during annual Military Appreciation events and discounted season passes. Also partners with Armed Forces Entertainment for additional discounts. Varies by park location.
how_to_verify: Military ID at gate during appreciation events. Check SixFlags.com/military for current offers.
notes: Discount amount and event timing varies significantly by park location. Confirm with your local Six Flags before planning a visit around the military discount.

org: America the Beautiful — Military Annual Pass
desc: Current U.S. military and their dependents get a completely free America the Beautiful Annual Pass valid at 2,000+ federal recreation sites — all national parks, national forests, national monuments, national wildlife refuges, BLM lands, Army Corps of Engineers recreation areas, and Bureau of Reclamation sites. Covers entrance fees for the entire vehicle.
how_to_verify: Obtain at any federal recreation site entrance, recreation.gov, or through base ITT office. CAC or dependent military ID required.
notes: One of the most underutilized benefits. $80 value per year for unlimited access to Grand Canyon, Yellowstone, Yosemite, Zion, Acadia, and 1,990+ other sites. Covers the whole car — not just the military member.

discount: Free Admission (always free — no military ID needed)
desc: All 19 Smithsonian museums and galleries in Washington D.C. and New York City are permanently free to all visitors. No military discount needed — they're always free. Includes: National Air and Space Museum, National Museum of American History, National Museum of Natural History, National Zoo, American Art Museum, and more.
how_to_verify: No verification needed — free to everyone. No tickets required.
notes: Worth noting as a D.C. area benefit — the Smithsonian Air and Space Museum is one of the most visited in the world and is directly relevant to military aviation history and culture.

discount: Free to low-cost comprehensive health care
desc: TRICARE provides comprehensive health coverage for the military community. Active duty: free coverage with no copays at MTFs. Retirees under 65: TRICARE Prime (~$325/yr individual premium) or Select. Reserve/Guard: TRICARE Reserve Select (~$52/month individual). Dental and vision plans separately available. Prescription coverage through the TRICARE Pharmacy network.
how_to_verify: Eligibility verified via DEERS. Use beneficiary portal at tricare.mil to find providers and manage coverage.
notes: TRICARE for Life (TFL) covers those 65+ with Medicare Part B as a supplement. TRICARE Overseas Program (TOP) covers active duty and their families stationed OCONUS.

desc: Many Anytime Fitness locations offer a military discount of $5–$15/month. As a franchise model, participation varies by club. 24/7 access at 5,000+ locations worldwide including international. Key cards allow access at any location globally.
how_to_verify: Military ID at enrollment. Discount varies by location — call ahead or ask at signup.
notes: Franchise model means rates vary widely. For active duty stationed on a base, the on-base fitness center is free — Anytime Fitness is most useful for veterans/retirees without base access.

desc: Planet Fitness offers military discounts at most locations. The standard Classic membership is already $10/month; the Black Card (home + guest privileges at all locations) is ~$25/month. Military discount shaves $1–$3 off. Nationwide 2,400+ locations.
how_to_verify: Military ID at membership enrollment.
notes: Planet Fitness already has very low rates — the military discount is modest but the underlying price is excellent for budget-conscious veterans.

discount: Up to 100% tuition + BAH housing allowance + $1,000/yr books
desc: The Post-9/11 GI Bill covers tuition and fees up to the state's highest public school rate, a monthly housing allowance equivalent to BAH at the E-5 with dependent rate, and $1,000/year for books and supplies. Maximum 36 months of benefits. Transferable to dependents after 6 years of service with a 4-year commitment. 100% benefits require 36+ months of aggregate service after 9/10/2001.
how_to_verify: Apply at VA.gov/education. Certificate of Eligibility (COE) issued after approval. Submit to school's VA certifying official.
notes: The housing allowance for full-time students can be $1,500–$3,500+/month depending on school location. File your Intent to Use to lock in your eligibility date.

desc: Tuition Assistance covers 100% of tuition costs for active duty up to $250 per semester hour and $4,500/year, for classes taken during off-duty time. Does not affect GI Bill entitlement. Each service branch manages its own TA program with slightly different rules. Navy: for military.navy.mil; Army: ArmyIgnitED; Air Force: myEducation; Marines: MarineNet.
how_to_verify: Apply through your branch's education portal. Supervisor/commander approval required.
notes: Critical planning tip: using TA does NOT consume GI Bill entitlement. Many separating servicemembers use TA while on active duty and save their GI Bill for graduate school or post-separation use.

discount: 1 year free LinkedIn Learning + LinkedIn Premium Career
desc: LinkedIn offers 1 year of free LinkedIn Learning access (20,000+ courses in tech, business, and creative skills) plus LinkedIn Premium Career to active duty and veterans. LinkedIn Premium Career normally costs $40/month. Excellent for transition planning, certification prep, and building a post-service professional network.
how_to_verify: Apply at LinkedIn.com/military with military email or DD-214 documentation.
notes: LinkedIn Premium Career enables InMail, applicant insights, and salary data — highly valuable during transition job searching. The free year is a one-time benefit.

discount: Free cloud training and certification prep
desc: AWS Educate and AWS re/Start programs provide free cloud computing training, AWS certification prep, and job placement support specifically for military veterans and transitioning servicemembers. The AWS Cloud Practitioner and Solutions Architect certifications are high-value credentials in the civilian tech job market.
how_to_verify: Apply at AWS.amazon.com/training/military with veteran status verification.
notes: AWS certifications command $90K–$150K+ starting salaries. The re/Start program is a 12-week full-time bootcamp with job placement support — one of the highest-ROI veteran transition programs available.

desc: Monthly tax-free payments to veterans with service-connected disabilities. Rates based on combined VA disability rating (0–100% in 10% increments). At 100% P&T (Permanent and Total): $3,831.30+/month as of 2024, adjusted annually by COLA. Rating effective from Intent to File date — file early. P&T status eliminates future re-examinations.
how_to_verify: File at VA.gov/disability or with VSO assistance. Intent to File preserves your effective date.
notes: File Intent to File NOW if approaching separation. The effective date of payment is the ITF date, not the claim approval date. VSO (DAV, VFW, American Legion) assistance is free and significantly improves claim outcomes.

discount: Zero down payment + no PMI + competitive rates
discount_pct: PMI savings alone = $1,000–$3,600/yr; down payment savings vary
desc: VA-backed home loans allow eligible veterans to buy, build, or refinance with no down payment and no private mortgage insurance. Competitive interest rates typically below conventional. VA funding fee waived entirely for veterans with any service-connected disability rating. Lifetime benefit — can be used multiple times.
how_to_verify: Obtain Certificate of Eligibility (COE) at VA.gov/housing-assistance/home-loans. Work with VA-approved lender.
notes: VA loan benefit can be used concurrently with a second VA loan in some cases (VA loan entitlement). Surviving spouses of veterans who died in service or from service-connected conditions are also eligible.

discount: Free to low-cost comprehensive VA health care
discount_pct: Free for priority groups 1–3; copays for groups 4–8
desc: VA healthcare provides comprehensive primary, specialty, mental health, dental, vision, and long-term care. Priority Groups 1–3 (combat veterans within 5 years of separation, disabled vets 10%+, low-income) receive free care. The VA is the largest integrated health care system in the U.S. with 1,200+ medical centers and outpatient clinics.
how_to_verify: Enroll at VA.gov/health-care or call 1-877-222-VETS.
notes: Combat veterans are eligible for 5 years of FREE VA health care from date of separation for any condition, not just service-connected ones. Enroll immediately — enrollment is not automatic.

discount: 10% first day, 2% exchange rewards, no foreign transaction fees
discount_pct: 10% first purchase day; 2% ongoing rewards at exchanges
desc: The Military Star card offers 10% off all purchases on your first day at military exchanges, 2% rewards on all Exchange purchases, and 1% on all other purchases worldwide. No annual fee. No foreign transaction fees. APR capped for military members. Can be used anywhere Mastercard accepted.
how_to_verify: Apply at Exchange stores or at MyECP.com/STAR. Military eligibility required.
notes: Strong choice for Exchange shoppers. No foreign transaction fees makes it useful for OCONUS tours. The 2% exchange rewards stack with existing exchange tax-free pricing.

discount_pct: ~70% below comparable civilian term life rates
desc: SGLI provides up to $400,000 of group life insurance coverage at just $0.07 per $1,000 of coverage ($28/month for $400K). Automatic enrollment for active duty. Trauma insurance (TSGLI) provides additional lump-sum payments for severe injuries. Transitional SGLI (TSGLI) available for 1–2 years post-separation.
how_to_verify: Automatically enrolled. Manage coverage and beneficiaries at benefits.va.gov/insurance.
notes: Transition critical: SGLI coverage ends 120 days after separation. Purchase Veterans' Group Life Insurance (VGLI) within 240 days of separation to convert without medical underwriting.
